delta.key_difference: Skill agent got clean builds on first attempt; baseline needed 3 error-fix iterations on Test 1 (76s vs 181s). Both ultimately passed all assertions.

discriminating_metrics: Duration on Test 1: 76s (with_skill) vs 181s (baseline) — 2.4x faster. Skill guidance prevented syntax errors that required multiple fix rounds.
First-attempt success: with_skill succeeded first try on all tests. Baseline needed 3 rounds on Test 1.
non_discriminating: Test 2 (harmony): confounded — baseline read existing proofs from repo. 44s baseline vs 55s with_skill.
Test 3 (rank-nullity): comparable quality. Both agents found correct Mathlib types. with_skill used DivisionRing (more general), baseline used Field.
observations: The skill's value is in EFFICIENCY, not just correctness — both can eventually get there, but the skill reduces the error-fix loop.
The with_skill agent produced more focused output (46 lines vs 69 on Test 1, 4 theorems vs 7 on Test 3), suggesting the skill's 'keep it boring' guidance channels effort productively.
The with_skill claim.md files are more structured (Phase 1 template: Objects/Properties/Proof sketch/Decomposition).
Neither agent could run Bash from subagent context for Tests 1-2 with_skill — need to grant Bash permissions to subagents or add verification in the main context.
